_meta.purpose: r10 vs r9 hire_sim regression root cause analysis (lenient judge, same endpoint)
_meta.author: nexus research agent (r10_regression)

r10_metrics.model_label_in_json: ALM-14B-r9-LoRA (mis-labeled; runner string not updated — actual adapter per alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json is ckpt_alm_14b_r10)

domain_split_interpretation: Deterministic-output domains (code, schedule) fully preserved. Free-form text domains (email, meeting, doc, research) all lost completion. Research hit hardest (-0.4 rate / -0.2 score). This splits cleanly along 'structured vs narrative output' — the exact pattern expected from LoRA over-fit to templated synthetic corpus.

candidate_root_causes.hypothesis: LoRA adapter drift from over-training on tiny templated synthetic corpus
candidate_root_causes.evidence: training/deploy/alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:22-28 — final_loss 7.82, best_eval 7.71, loss_ema 7.84. Base inference loss for Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct is ~2.0. Loss never dropped below 7.7 across 1500 steps despite starting at 17.4 — training trajectory shows adapter was diverging from base distribution, not converging to task. Corpus was 300 records × ~200KB generated from only 30 seed tasks × 10 variants (r10_training_plan.md:12-15). Response length inflated +18% (+164 chars mean) while keyword hits DROPPED — classic verbosity drift from template mimicry.

candidate_root_causes.hypothesis: LoRA-on-LoRA stacking with excessive rank (r=16 on top of r9 r=32)
candidate_root_causes.evidence: alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:57 — 'lora_r=16 + alpha=32 on top of already-trained r9 LoRA stacking — effective rank too high, drift compounded'. r9 used lora_r=32/alpha=64 (r10_training_plan.md:36). Stacking without lr reduction compounds drift. The 2e-6 lr was 'low for vanilla but high for stacking' (alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:84).

r10_training_plan.md:164-165 — 'Keyword diversity is narrow (10-13 unique keywords per domain) — the model may memorize the keyword set rather than generalize the augmentation pattern.' Per-domain regressions align: email/meeting/research (where synth templates were most repetitive) dropped most; code/schedule (natively structured) survived. Model learned verbose templated style but NOT which keywords matter.

candidate_root_causes.hypothesis: Hot LoRA swap without endpoint restart (violates reference_alm_serve_api)
candidate_root_causes.evidence: Endpoint identical in both runs (itfl66q4z768kh:8090). r10_eval_plan_20260416.md:41-48 shows spinning up a SEPARATE port 8091 for r10 — but BOTH r9 and r10 JSONs record endpoint :8090. alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:61 confirms 'Reverted live serve (itfl66q4z768kh:8090) to /runpod/ckpt_r9' — meaning r10 was swapped into 8090 then reverted. If swap used hot-reload instead of kill+restart, adapter state could be partially applied. Less likely to cause domain-specific regression pattern though.

No temperature field in either JSON; both runs show net_status=ok. Latency distributions essentially identical (9.18s vs 9.19s). Non-determinism would produce noise, not the observed systematic structured-vs-narrative split.

most_likely_cause: LoRA-on-LoRA over-training on small templated synthetic corpus (hypotheses 1+2+3 combined). r10 = r9 + 1500 steps × lr=2e-6 × lora_r=16 on 300-record synthetic corpus with CE-only loss. Final loss 7.82 (vs base ~2.0) shows adapter diverged from instruction-following distribution. Free-form domains lost 0.2-0.4 completion rate while structured domains held — classic capability forgetting signature. Output verbosity inflated +18% without corresponding keyword gain = model learned templated fluff, not task.
verification_plan: 1. Ask the ALM pod owner (sibling agent) to confirm whether 8090 currently serves r9 or r10 — alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:61 says reverted to r9 already. 2. If r10 LoRA still on disk at /workspace/ckpt_alm_14b_r10, rerun hire_sim_lenient against a FRESH process (kill+restart) to rule out hot-swap artifacts. 3. If completion stays <0.80, confirm catastrophic-forget diagnosis and proceed to r11 plan (alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:67-80 — lr=5e-7, 500 steps, lora_r=8, 80% korean general + 20% hire_sim mix). 4. Log r10 final loss trajectory (17.4 → 7.82) as ossified anti-pattern in shared/state/runpod_ossified.
recommendation_rationale: r9 lenient 0.8667 already PASSED the ALM 0.85 gate (committed d6e6de87). r10 underperforms r9 on 4/6 domains without improving the 2 deterministic domains. Per feedback_no_resume_data_change: 'Changing training data requires fresh start from step 0 — never --resume'. r10 violated this by stacking LoRA on r9's trained adapter with a radically different (templated synthetic) corpus. r10 should be retired and r9 promoted as canonical ALM-14B v2.0 RC. r11 should be a FRESH LoRA run from base (not r9) per the plan in alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:67-80.
author_notes: The r10 JSON's model label still says 'ALM-14B-r9-LoRA' — this is a runner-side string that was not updated when the adapter was swapped. The alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json file confirms the adapter served IS r10 (ckpt_alm_14b_r10).
Both JSONs were generated 42 minutes apart (10:17Z → 10:59Z); no data/corpus/judge changes between them — only adapter swap.
r10 preserved on disk per alm_r10_regression_diagnosis.json:62 — safe to forensically re-eval if needed.
